Pentagon considers diverting Ukraine military aid to the Middle East, Washington Post reports
data.description:
March 26 (Reuters) - The Pentagon is considering whether to divert weapons intended for Ukraine to the Middle East as the war in Iran depletes some of the U.S. military's most critical munitions, the Washington Post reported on Thursday, citing three people familiar with the matter.

(Reporting by Shivani Tanna in Bengaluru, Editing by Louise Heavens)

Prologis CEO Sees Prolonged AI, Data Center Tailwinds
data.description:
Prologis (PLD) CEO Dan Letter joins Bloomberg Businessweek Daily to discuss the state of global logistics and shipping as geopolitics and associated risk impacts the firm's customer base. However, Prologis is optimistic about its data center business. Letter says the company's strong relationship with hyperscalers is ongoing, bolstering "a fortress of a balance sheet" as Prologis continues to secure longer-term power capacity. Letter speaks with Carol Massar and Tim Stenovec.

Bloomberg videos, provided by MT Newswires

data.title: South Korea to invest $166 million in AI chip startup Rebellions
Back in the USA - Russian lawmakers make first visit for years
data.description:
MOSCOW, March 26 (Reuters) - A delegation of Russian lawmakers has arrived in the United States for meetings with U.S. lawmakers, Russian media said, on the first such trip since relations between the world's two biggest nuclear powers hit rock bottom over the war in Ukraine.

After the 2022 invasion of Ukraine, Russian officials said relations with Washington were worse than at any time in living memory. Ties have improved since President Donald Trump returned to the White House and sought to end the war.

The lawmakers' visit to the United States, first reported by Russia's Vedomosti newspaper, includes Vyacheslav Nikonov, a lawmaker who is the grandson of Josef Stalin's foreign minister, Vyacheslav Molotov, according to Russian media reports.

The Izvestia newspaper cited the U.S. embassy in Moscow as saying Russian lawmakers would meet U.S. lawmakers on Thursday and then meet U.S. officials on Friday.

The visit is "part of the normalisation of relations with the United States of America," Alexei Chepa, first deputy chairman of the State Duma Committee on International Affairs, told Izvestia.

Russian media reported that a special Russian flight had landed in Washington - and that the Russian lawmakers were believed to be on the flight. Reuters was unable to immediately confirm the reports.

Republican U.S. Representative Anna Paulina Luna said in January that she had received authorisation from the State Department for four Russian lawmakers to meet members of Congress.

Closer ties between Washington and Moscow are a concern for Ukraine and its European backers, but have been welcomed by the Kremlin.

The U.S. is making an offer of security guarantees for a peace deal in Ukraine conditional on Kyiv ceding all of the eastern region of Donbas to Russia, President Volodymyr Zelenskiy told Reuters in an interview.

(Reporting by Guy Faulconbridge, Editing by Timothy Heritage)

data.title: Rohm, Toshiba, Mitsubishi Elec to begin power chip integration talks, Nikkei says
South Korea asks companies to cooperate on foreign-exchange stability
LIC's Momentum Builds Amid Market Drift
data.description:
The Indian insurance industry has travelled a transformative path—from the nationalization of life and nonlife segments in 1956 and 1972 respectively, to a vibrant market of 74 insurers today. Backed by regulatory reforms and rising private participation, the sector recorded total premium income of 82.5 billion US dollars in FY 25, growing steadily despite global volatility. With India emerging as the fourth largest general insurance market in Asia, the industry now stands at a defining point of expansion.

Across categories, insurance consumption is rising, supported by digital adoption, new distribution models, and government schemes. Insurance penetration reached 3.7% in FY 24, with life insurance at 2.8% and non-life insurance at 0.9%, while density rose to USD 95 per capita. Non-life premiums grew 7.3% in H1 26, driven by health, motor and crop insurance.

Private players strengthened their grip, lifting nonlife market share to 65.4% in FY 25. Digital platforms such as Bima Sugam and IoT-based underwriting are further broadening access nationwide.

The outlook remains upbeat as India’s insurance market is projected to reach USD 222bn by FY 26, powered by favorable regulation, expanding middleclass incomes, and technology-led efficiency. High growth segments—crop insurance, health premiums, and surging motor insurance linked to record vehicle sales of 4.3 million units—signal broad based opportunity.

With 62% of new premiums now coming from smaller towns and ambitious reforms targeting "Insurance for All by 2047", the sector is set for multiyear expansion.

Amid this dynamic market, LIC remains the industry anchor with a commanding 57.1% share of premiums and premium collections of USD 26.2bn in FY 25. Its strong brand value of USD 9.8bn and consistent renewal premium growth reinforces its dominance even as private insurers scale rapidly. In June 2025, LIC held 63.5% market share, collecting USD 3.2bn (+3.4% y/y) in premiums, underscoring its unparalleled reach across India’s insurance landscape.

Premiums power ahead

Over 9M 26, LIC’s top line kept its cadence, with total premium income rising to INR 3,712.9bn, up 9% y/y—a lift powered by resilient renewal premiums (+6.8% y/y), firmer individual New Business Premium (NBP) (+5.9% y/y), and a robust group business (+13.6% y/y). Mix tailwinds helped too: the non-par share of individual NBP by premium climbed to 60.5%, while bancassurance/alternate channels’ NBP jumped 66.7%.

Profitability outpaced revenue: PAT advanced to INR 340bn (+16.7% y/y), underpinned by a leaner overall expense ratio (11.7% vs 13%) and lower commission ratio (4.5% vs 5.2%). The policyholders’ account surplus—a useful operating proxy—improved to INR 320.5bn (+10% y/y), aided by steady investment income and better persistency. Asset quality also firmed, with gross NPA at 1.3%.

Optimistic outlook

Despite delivering steady results, the company witnessed its shares tumble 6.5% over the past 12 months, bringing its market capitalization down to INR 4,940bn (USD 52.7bn). Interestingly, the stock now trades at a forward P/E of just 8.8x based on 2027 earnings estimates—a significant discount to its three-year average of 11.3x.

The Street's consensus paints a decidedly optimistic picture: out of 21 analysts, 17 analysts recommend 'Buy' against merely three 'Hold' ratings. Their average target price of INR 1,089.6 (USD 11.5) suggests a compelling 39.1% upside from current levels. The most bullish forecasts are even more striking; some analysts envision the stock reaching INR 1,450.0 (USD 15.4), implying a remarkable 85.6% appreciation potential from today's price.

Risks loom

LIC stands at a crossroads—while its operational momentum remains intact, the road ahead isn't without challenges. Rising competition from agile private insurers threatens its market dominance, while evolving customer preferences demands faster digital transformation.

Regulatory shifts and margin pressures from product mix changes could test profitability. Asset quality concerns and persistence issues loom quietly in the background. Yet for investors willing to navigate these headwinds, the current valuation disconnect presents an intriguing opportunity—if the company can successfully modernize while defending its legacy stronghold.

data.title: Google top India counsel quits in latest departure amid regulatory hurdles, sources say
data.description:
NEW DELHI, March 26 (Reuters) - Google's top India counsel, Bijoya Roy, has resigned after 16 months in the role, two sources said, a high-profile exit in a key market where the U.S. tech giant is facing regulatory hurdles and also lacks a government relations head.

India is crucial for Alphabet's Google since most smartphones in the country run on its Android operating system, even as Apple's share is growing steadily.

Google also faces antitrust cases in India, legal challenges over AI training and stricter-than-ever content takedown regulations that started applying to tech companies from February.

Roy quit last month for personal reasons to start her own venture, said one of the sources on Thursday. The two sources declined to be named as the decision is not public.

Google did not respond to a request for comment, while Roy declined to comment.

Last year, Google's head of public policy in India, Sreenivasa Reddy, quit, the second departure for that role in around two years. The company has still not filled the role.

In October, Google said it would invest $15 billion over five years to set up an artificial intelligence data centre in India's southern state of Andhra Pradesh, its biggest ever investment in the world's most populous nation.

(Reporting by Aditya Kalra; Editing by Arun Koyyur)

Jefferies raises Nokia price target, sees conservative guidance
data.description:
Jefferies has raised its price target for Nokia to 8.8 euros from 7.2 euros, citing signals of stronger market growth in optical networking, according to a research note.

The bank points out that during the OFC industry conference, the company upgraded its outlook for the addressable market compared to its Capital Markets Day last November. Analysts see potential for market share gains driven by product portfolio strength, strategic partnerships, and capacity expansion.

Company forecasts are considered conservative, and Jefferies expects them to be exceeded, particularly from 2027 onwards as a new manufacturing facility begins contributing to growth.

Buy rating reiterated.
